stores.title: Thermaltake The Tower 300 m-ATX Micro Tower Gaming Computer Case, Black (CA-1Y4-00S1WN-00)

about_the_product.description: As a new member of the Tower series, The Tower 300 shares the same silhouette with its predecessors, offering full display to the system components. The most obvious appearance between its families is the octagon design. You can fit 4090 GPU and a 420mm radiator in The Tower 300, keeping the motion high and the temperature low. Besides two preinstalled top fans, you can also install two 120/140mm behind the motherboard or one 120/140mm fan on top of the power cover or three 120/140mm fans at the right side fan bracket. It provides excellent hardware expandability to accommodates latest PC hardware without compensating the building experience.

user_reviews.title: Make sure your GPU doesn't use a vapor chamber
user_reviews.text:
A case with a relatively small footprint, good airflow (with additional fans) and great visual appeal. It fit my 360mm radiator with no problems.

However, a couple of things to note: All of your ports will be inside the case, which makes (dis)connecting devices and cables a hassle. In addition, take care when selecting a gpu to pair with it. If your graphics card has a vapor chamber, it will probably hate sitting vertically. This causes serious thermal issues that may look like a faulty cooler. My card works fine with Thermaltake's horizontal stand.

user_reviews.title: Its a decent case all in all but with one downside
user_reviews.text: The case is pretty spacious for an mATX and slightly bigger than your average mATX ! The bottom and back compartments offer excellent cable management , making it a pretty clean chasy . But the downside is that the motherboard's I/O panel is fully internal making a more frequent access to the PC ports a little more tedious , requiring to remove the top lid and routing them via the back cable rourting cut-outs ! Also the fan tray under the top lid works like a hatch that can be opened to get to the cable ports so i guess that partly compensates the lack of external access for the ports , but only partly as its still a bit tedious , specially if you plan to put your rig in a poorly acessible spot !

PC Case with different takes on the orientation of the internal component, looks super slick despite of the unconventional component layout and a bulkier in size for an M-ATX. The huge size makes up for its cooling capability even when only using air cooler on the CPU instead of AIO

If you are bored with the classic layout of your PC, this definitely a fun one and also fun to build.

Also note to the seller:
As a collector, I would prefer the box to not be altered/tempered with the shipping stickers but it's really a minor issue. No damages when the item was received due to all of the warnings on the product box, highly appreciated with the precautionary steps taken by the vendor.

user_reviews.title: A very well built case that looks different
user_reviews.text:
I wanted a case that wasn't just the usual Front & Side panel glass in a rectangular box and thought I'd try this. This isn't my first thermaltake case with my previous being a Level 20VT (All sides tempered glass).

This was a fairly enjoyable building experience, there are a few areas where it's a squish getting it in however the cable management in the rear of the machine helps out a lot.

This one resolves an issue I had with my previous where the rear compartment is fully hidden / covered up so no cables are visible at all.

Admittidly it was bigger than I expected it to be considering it's an MATX case. However it does then mean it fits a full size MATX Motherboard (this sounds odd but a few cases I've used only fit a smaller / shorter variant of MATX). A fairly big GPU (ASUS 4070 Ti TUF) and full size PSU.

There's a lot of mesh on every fan filter so hopefully should be good with reducing dust.

The only quirk is that you have to route any cables to the motherboard through the case too which whilst in my case is fine as I have minimal things connected. If I was needing to use every IO port this would be a tad of a pain.

user_reviews.title: Gereat Smaller Case With A Different Look
user_reviews.text:
Despite being a bit larger for an mATX case it is well laid out and can accommodate some larger components with ease.

Bought this to move from a Lian Li V3000 PLUS which is a bit of downsizing to say the least.

With the right fans this case is great for airflow and cooling. I have built a custom loop in mine which needs the right pump/res combination to make work without looking too crowded.

Running a 3080Ti and Ryzen 7900 temps with a 420mm rad are great and airflow across the rest is enough to keep everything cool.

The case is well made, and with all the removable panels makes building an ease. One thing that could be improved is the pass through areas around the motherboard EPS connector area and the rear SSD bracket. For the price it's a very good case with only a few minor issues that are easily worked around.

There is plenty of room in the bottom for larger PSU's although modern units are a lot more compact than they used to be. The extra space is much needed for hiding excess cabling, especially any GPU cables as these are a really short run.

I will be getting the stand kit when available and have built my system to work in either orientation in advance.

user_reviews.title: Well thought out, well made, and… so very cute!
user_reviews.text:
I saw this case on the CES 2024 videos and immediately wanted one. My gaming PCs for the past three decades have always been full-tower, ATX monsters so this would be my first mATX gaming build. I have to say I am super-impressed by the design, the build quality, finish and how cute it looks!
The attention to detail is incredible — everything fits exactly where it is supposed to, with every internal panel individually clipped and screwed into place so that they could be completely reconfigured to suit my desired layout.
All cutouts, cable-tie anchors and flexible cable clips have been thoughtfully placed in all the right places, and even though there is only one main cable grommet between the main and secondary chambers, none of the other cutouts present sharp edges to damage my cables. In fact, the finish is so smooth I actually removed the cable grommet because a) I didn't need it and b) the black rubber didn't match the stark white interior powder coating…!
I could fit all my components inside with ease, with plenty of room to get my hands inside, despite this being mATX form-factor. Admittedly, this case is larger than what you might expect from an mATX case but it is still smaller than all of my other full towers.
I can easily fit the chonky-boi ASUS ROG STRYX 4090 OC inside, and the vertical orientation means I don't even need to fit a support. The case has space for an extraordinary number of 140mm fans as well as a triple-fan AIO CPU cooler. My only gripe is having to settle for a 360mm radiator because nobody makes a 420mm AIO in white. This mATX form factor case has been designed for some serious cooling.

My setup: 7800X3D, RTX 4090 OC 24GB, 32GB DDR5, 2x 4TB Gen4 SSD, 1x 1TB Gen5 SSD, 1200W ATX3.0 PSU.

user_reviews.title: Thermaltake Tower 300 review
user_reviews.text: Great case, it’s a little fiddly routing the power supply cables through but doable, get a modular power supply so you only have the cables you need to use and not excess to tie up. If you can afford it get an AIO fans/heatsink. I didn’t and I’m a bit worried over time that the heatsink weight and gravity may warp the mobo. It would be nice if Thermatake had made an extension cable set to come off the I/O ports like you get with the line in cables for TV’s, this so you could easily access the I/O ports instead of having to have the top off to plug things in. Thankfully I had a spare HDMI extender cable lying around. All openings have dust shields which is a good feature. I would have added some pics but the option was greyed out.
